div.p: In standard pointers, A == A' based on memory address alone. In tagged pointers, (v:0, A) != (v:3, A').
The 128-bit atomic CAS ensures that if any thread modifies the stack, the version counter must change. Even if the address is reused, the tuple remains unique across time.
Thread 1 Resumes Attempts to commit stale state
top = {v:0, ptr:A} Stack: A -> B -> C
